title: Monty Betham
wikitext:
{{Short description|New Zealand & Samoa international rugby league footballer}} {{For|his father, the boxer|Monty Betham (boxer)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=January 2015}} {{Use New Zealand English|date=January 2015}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Monty Betham |fullname = {{nowrap|La’auli Montgomery Junior Betham}}[http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=B&id=691 ] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120402171709/http://nzleague.co.nz/kiwis/player_profile.php?letter=B&id=691|date=2 April 2012}} |image = |caption = |retired = yes |position = {{rlp|HK|SR|LK}} |birth_date = {{birth date and age|1978|3|12|df=yes}} |birth_place = [[Auckland]], New Zealand |height = {{convert|183|cm|ftin|abbr=on}} |weight = {{convert|94|kg|stlb|abbr=on}} |club1 = [[New Zealand Warriors]] |year1start = 1999 |year1end = 05 |appearances1 = 101 |tries1 = 10 |goals1 = 0 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 40 |club2 = {{nowrap|[[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]}} |year2start = 2006 |year2end = |appearances2 = 26 |tries2 = 2 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 0 |points2 = 8 |teamA = [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] |yearAstart = 2000 |yearAend = |appearancesA = 4 |triesA = 1 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 4 |teamB = [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] |yearBstart = 2001 |yearBend = 06 |appearancesB = 9 |triesB = 1 |goalsB = 0 |fieldgoalsB = 0 |pointsB = 4 |updated = |source = {{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/monty-betham/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}} | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = [[Monty Betham (boxer)|Monty Betham Sr.]] | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = }} }} '''La’auli Montgomery Junior "Monty" Betham''' (born 12 March 1978) is a professional [[boxing|boxer]], and former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A New Zealand international representative {{rlp|hk}} and {{rlp|lk}}, he played club football for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] ([[Captain (sports)|captain]]) in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Betham was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, on 12 March 1978. His father, Samoan-born [[Monty Betham Sr.]], had 53 professional fights as a [[middleweight]] or [[light heavyweight]] from 1973-1982. ==Rugby league career== Betham was a [[Bay Roskill Vikings]] junior, he was selected to play for Samoa at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].{{Cite web|url=http://aucklandleague.co.nz/display_page.asp?item=359|archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20100523170735/http://aucklandleague.co.nz/display_page.asp?item=359|archive-date = 23 May 2010|title = Auckland Rugby League - Home}} Betham went on to change his international allegiance and represent [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in eight tests His position of choice was at {{rlp|lk}} but he also played at {{rlp|hk}} and {{rlp|sr}}.{{cite news|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/view/page/410965/481365|title=Warriors profile: Monty Betham|date=24 March 2005|work=[[Television New Zealand]]|access-date=26 October 2011}} On the field Betham was known for his aggressive style of play. He was once voted the player that the opposition players least want to pick a fight with in an NRL players' poll. Betham missed New Zealand's first ever finals appearance during the [[2001 NRL season]] and the [[2002 NRL Grand Final]] due to injury. Betham's final game for the club was a 22-20 victory over Manly at [[Brookvale Oval]] in round 25 of the [[2005 NRL season]]. Betham spent one year in England with [[Wakefield Trinity]] during the [[2006 Super League season]].{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sunday-news/sport/league/2928698/Don-t-play-it-again-Daniel/|title=Don't play it again, Daniel|website=www.stuff.co.nz|date=3 October 2009}} === Highlights === *''Junior Club:'' Papatoetoe & Bay Roskill *''First Grade Debut:'' Round 1, Auckland v Sydney City at Aussie Stadium, 8 March 1999 *''Career Stats:'' 101 career games to date scoring 10 tries *''Professional Boxing Debut:'' On 31 March 2006 Monty defeated Vai Toevai via sixth round KO in Apia, Samoa. *''Professional Boxing Record'' - 5-0-0 ==Boxing career== In December 2006, Betham announced his retirement from [[rugby league]] in order to pursue a career in [[boxing]]. Betham will start out in the [[Cruiserweight (boxing)|cruiserweight]] division and hopes to work his way down to [[light heavyweight]]. His first fight took place in Samoa on 31 March 2007. He defeated 15 fight veteran Vai Toevai in the sixth round.[http://www.samoaobserver.ws/sports/SPNews/0407/0107sp001.htm ] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070928145903/http://www.samoaobserver.ws/sports/SPNews/0407/0107sp001.htm|date=28 September 2007}} In 2013, an episode of [[The X Factor (New Zealand series 1)]] was filmed at [[Boxing Alley]] gym featuring Monty Betham and contestant [[Whenua Patuwai]]. As well as being a professional boxer, he also trained [[Daniella Smith]] who went on to become IBF World Champion.{{Cite web |date=2023-02-10 |title='I broke down in tears': Daniella Smith stunned by her induction to Women's Boxing Hall of Fame|url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/combat-sports/300802163/i-broke-down-in-tears-daniella-smith-stunned-by-her-induction-to-womens-boxing-hall-of-fame |access-date=2023-12-18 |publisher=Stuff NZ |language=en}} ==Professional boxing record== {{S-start}} | style="text-align:center;" colspan="8"|'''8 Wins''' (5 knockouts, 3 decisions), '''1 Losses''', '''0 Draws''' |- ! [[Tuanaimato|Faleata Sports Complex]], [[Apia]], [[Tuamasaga]]}} |align=left|{{small|Won vacant Samoa [[Cruiserweight (boxing)|Cruiserweight]] title.
Professional debut.}} |} ===Title=== ''Regional/International Titles:'' *NZNBF [[Cruiserweight (boxing)|Cruiserweight]] Champion ''(197 lbs)'' *Samoa [[Cruiserweight (boxing)|Cruiserweight]] Champion ''(196 lbs)'' ==Television== Betham was runner-up on Season 4 of [[Dancing with the Stars (New Zealand TV series)|New Zealand Dancing With The Stars]] in 2008. Betham is trained by Danny Codling and managed by [[Mick Watson]]. In 2007 Betham competed in New Zealand reality show ''[[Treasure Island (show)|Treasure Island:Pirates of the Pacific]]''.{{cite news|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/view/page/411307/979149|title=Treasure Island: Pirates of the Pacific|work=[[Television New Zealand]]|access-date=26 October 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110516164118/http://tvnz.co.nz/view/page/411307/979149|archive-date=16 May 2011|df=dmy}} In March 2008 he competed on ''New Zealand Celebrity Joker Poker'', he made it to the final three on the final episode before being eliminated (Shortland Street actor Craig Parker was the overall winner). ===''Dancing with the Stars'' (New Zealand, Season 4)=== In December 2007 it was announced that Betham would compete in season 4 of ''[[Dancing with the Stars (New Zealand TV series)|Dancing with the Stars]]'' which started in February 2008.{{cite news|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/view/page/497100/1505760|title=2008 celebs announced...|work=[[Television New Zealand]]|access-date=26 October 2011}}{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/topic/story.cfm?c_id=169&objectid=10482664|title=NZ celebs set aside fears in the name of dance|date=16 December 2007|agency=[[NZPA]]|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=26 October 2011}} On 28 January 2008 it was revealed on [[The Edge (New Zealand radio station)|The Edge (radio station)]] that Betham's professional dance partner would be Nerida Jantti (girlfriend of [[Shane Cortese]]). On 15 April 2008 he was named runner-up ([[Temepara George]] being the overall winner). ===Filmography=== *''[[Treasure Island: Pirates of the Pacific]]'' (2007) *''Dancing With The Stars'' (Season 4, 2008) *''Celebrity Joker Poker'' (16–30 March 2008) ===The Shopping Channel=== In October 2012 Betham became one of the inaugural TV presenters on [[The Shopping Channel (New Zealand)]], New Zealand's first and only [[Shopping channel|home shopping channel]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/nz/news/article.cfm?c_id=1&objectid=10816577|title=Shopping for new channel hosts - National - NZ Herald News|date=9 February 2012 |publisher=Nzherald.co.nz|access-date=2015-10-16}} ===Monty Betham's Steps for Life Foundation=== With his sister Chante Betham-Spencer (CEO) he founded '''Monty Betham's Steps for Life Foundation''' in 2010 to "Help NZ youth and families in the fight against childhood obesity in NZ".{{cite web| url=http://www.stepsforlife.co.nz/| title=Monty Betham's Steps for Life Foundation|publisher=Stepsforlife.co.nz| access-date=26 October 2014}} ==Personal life== After returning home from competing on Treasure Island, Betham and his wife Jaymie celebrated the birth of their first child, a son.{{cite news|url=http://tvnz.co.nz/view/page/434905/982254|title=Monty Betham|work=[[Television New Zealand]]|access-date=26 October 2011|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110516164501/http://tvnz.co.nz/view/page/434905/982254|archive-date=16 May 2011|df=dmy}} Betham's wife is currently expecting their second child. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Further reading== *{{cite book | last = Matheson | first = John | title = Monty Betham: baring my soul | publisher = HarperCollins | year = 2008 | location =
